method.description: Paired baseline and perceptual-augmented findings were written as evaluation fixtures to isolate how the experimental hint layer changes findings prose.
method.weak_generic_brand_source: Example Company / example.com is available in existing benchmark/output artifacts and is used as the intentionally weak or generic case.

paired_outputs.baseline_narrative: Apple's public surface presents a highly polished product ecosystem with clear hierarchy, premium imagery, and consistent communication across devices and services. This supports a strong perception of coherence and reinforces the brand's leadership position in consumer technology.
paired_outputs.perceptual_augmented_narrative: Apple's owned surface repeatedly organizes attention around product object, material finish, controlled whitespace, and short declarative copy. That supports a high-confidence observation of product-first hierarchy; the broader reading of institutional authority remains an interpretation, not a directly visible fact. The productive tension sits between restraint and commercial density: the surface often feels minimal at the frame level while carrying many simultaneous product, ecosystem, and service signals.
strengths: Replaces reputation-led praise with visible mechanisms.
Names restraint and density as a productive tension.
weaknesses: Could still import reputation knowledge if evidence is not present.
overreach_flags: Do not claim authority, trust, or leadership unless corroborated.

paired_outputs.baseline_narrative: Linear presents a modern, focused SaaS experience with strong product clarity and a well-defined workflow narrative. The brand appears differentiated through a refined interface language and a clear emphasis on speed, collaboration, and execution.
paired_outputs.perceptual_augmented_narrative: Linear's available surface evidence points to product-navigation language, compact UI claims, and a controlled software-workflow frame. That supports a reading of guided movement rather than broad modern SaaS positioning: the surface directs attention through workflow, sequence, and operational rhythm. The claim/signal gap matters because some visual evidence is obstructed or screenshot-limited, so interface clarity should be written as a copy-supported reading until direct product-surface evidence confirms it.
strengths: Turns SaaS praise into guided movement.
Preserves evidence limitations around obstructed or screenshot-limited captures.
weaknesses: May become over-cautious for a mature brand when evidence is sparse.
overreach_flags: Do not assert product UX clarity from marketing copy alone.

paired_outputs.baseline_narrative: Stripe's documentation experience is robust and developer-friendly, with a comprehensive information architecture that supports onboarding and technical implementation. The brand demonstrates strong coherence between product complexity and developer trust.
paired_outputs.perceptual_augmented_narrative: Stripe Docs' surface is best read through operational density: navigation, code-oriented content, and implementation pathways make the category legible through use rather than campaign language. That supports a category-to-surface reading with a system-driven emotional temperature: controlled, dense, and instructional. The tension is not whether the brand is developer-friendly as a quality claim, but how much complexity the surface can expose while still guiding action.
strengths: Avoids trust claims and quality adjectives.
Names density and guidance as observable surface behaviors.
weaknesses: Operational density can become jargon if not anchored to visible navigation or code evidence.
overreach_flags: Do not infer developer trust without behavioral or third-party evidence.

paired_outputs.baseline_narrative: Headspace communicates a calm and accessible wellness brand through friendly visuals, approachable messaging, and a reassuring tone. The experience feels warm and supportive, helping users engage with meditation and mental health.
paired_outputs.perceptual_augmented_narrative: Headspace's surface evidence should separate stated wellness positioning from directly observed interface behavior. Soft color, simple hierarchy, and friendly illustration can support a low-to-medium confidence emotional temperature of calm accessibility, but supportive mental-health experience is an interpretation unless product-flow evidence is available. The useful tension is between emotional reassurance and proof of actual guidance: the surface may invite comfort while the evidence pool still needs interaction-level confirmation.
strengths: Separates wellness emotion from product efficacy.
weaknesses: Can become too procedural for a warm consumer wellness surface.
overreach_flags: Do not assert support, therapeutic impact, or user outcomes from surface tone.

paired_outputs.baseline_narrative: Notion has a cohesive productivity ecosystem with a flexible workspace narrative that appeals to teams and individuals. Its brand system is simple, recognizable, and effective at communicating versatility.
paired_outputs.perceptual_augmented_narrative: Notion's surface can be read as system cohesion with preserved difference when the evidence shows modular workspace language, template variety, and repeated block-based framing. The stronger observation is not that the ecosystem is cohesive, but that many use cases are held inside a shared workspace grammar. The tension sits between flexibility and legibility: the surface needs to show breadth without making the product feel like an undefined container.
strengths: Uses cohesion with preserved difference without treating cohesion as praise.
Names flexibility/legibility as a concrete tension.
weaknesses: Workspace grammar needs visible template, navigation, or product evidence.
overreach_flags: Do not claim adoption, workflow success, or ecosystem performance from copy.

paired_outputs.baseline_narrative: Example Company has a basic online presence with limited differentiation and generic messaging. The brand appears weak across presence and vitality, with little evidence of a distinctive market position.
paired_outputs.perceptual_augmented_narrative: Example Company's evidence pool appears thin and mostly self-referential, so the safest finding is an evidence-bound absence rather than a personality claim. The surface does not yet provide enough repeated signals to support a stable perceptual pattern. The narrative should name the limitation directly: Brand3 can observe low surface specificity and weak corroboration, but should not infer strategic failure or audience irrelevance from missing evidence alone.
strengths: Prevents weak-brand prose from becoming punitive.
Treats missing evidence as a limitation rather than a verdict.
overreach_flags: Do not infer failed strategy from absence of evidence.

where_augmentation_helps: Reduces generic praise and score-first framing.
Preserves low-confidence material as conditional or review-bound.
Improves tension quality by naming both sides of a perceptual relationship.
where_augmentation_harms: Can introduce abstract pattern vocabulary without enough evidence.
Can make familiar brands sound over-qualified when the local evidence pool is thin.
Can turn emotional temperature into a new generic adjective layer if not tied to mechanism.
Currently uses static lenses rather than brand-specific perceptual facts.
where_baseline_still_performs_better: Simpler summaries when evidence is already concrete.
Less risk of repeating registry vocabulary across brands.
too_interpretive_conditions: Authority, trust, support, calm, sophistication, or leadership appears without a visible mechanism.
Category-to-surface translation is treated as proof of strategy.
Emotional temperature is used as quality scoring.
Static pattern language is applied without brand evidence.

recommendation.summary: Keep the experiment opt-in. It improves narrative specificity when evidence anchors exist, but it is not yet safe as a default reporting layer.
before_production: Store baseline and augmented findings for identical evidence bundles.
Add checks for generic sophistication language.
Require one visible or source-level mechanism in every augmented finding.
Require conditional language for low-confidence or copy-based material.
Add a reviewer rubric for pattern vocabulary used without evidence.
Evaluate a larger paired corpus before any global enablement.
